rulings.Case Title: LISA TAFURI KRIM VS. CONSTRUCTIVE WORKS GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LISA KRIM, BRIAN KRIM Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal The request to set aside dismissal is denied as unnecessary. The Court does not need to vacate the dismissal in order to consider Plaintiffs' motion for entry of judgment. (Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal. App. 4th 429, 439-440.) Plaintiff may file a motion for entry of judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $578.OO U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 4, 2013, Line 1: Petitioner PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion for Order for Terminating Sanctions and Striking Claim; Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRET CORNELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Bret Cornell'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative Suggestion For Sua Sponte Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2013, Line 28, Plaintiff Bret Cornell'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative Suggestion For Sua Sponte Reconsideration Granted. The hearing will be at 9 am, not 9:30am in Department 302. The court exercises its discretion to reconsider the portion of the order filed July 20, 2012 granting summary adjudication in favor of defendants on the first cause of action and, upon reconsideration, vacates that portion of the order and enters a new order denying summary adjudication on the first cause of action. Bender explicitly holds that "where an arrest is unlawful and excessive force is applied in making the arrest, there has been coercion 'independent from the coercion inherent in the wrongful detention itself.'" (Emphasis in original). This holding, whether seen as a clarification of Shoyoye or new law, demonstrates that the prior ruling is in error and thus this court exercise its discretion to correct errors of law prior to entry of the final judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The hearing will be at 9 am, not 9:30am in Department 302. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: RALPH BARBIERI VS. CUMULUS MEDIA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Pltf'S Summary Judgment Opposition; Memo Of Pts And Auth; Decs Of Elizabeht Bulat Turner And Lee Hammer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT CUMULUS MEDIA INC., CUMULUS MEDIA PARTNERS LLC, CUMULUS - SAN FRANCISCO, CMP SUSQUEHANNA CORP'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Plaitiff's Summary Judgment Opposition Granted in part. No opposition filed. The Court finds good cause to seal all of the requested documents except items 4 (Attachment B to Second Request for Admission to Lee Hammer, Ex. G to the Robinson Declaration) and 5 (Attachment B to Second Request for Admission to Susquehanna Radio Corp., Ex. I to the Robinson Declaration). As to the remaining documents, the Court finds that the documents contain commercially sensitive business information and/or confidential trade secrets. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records, the overriding interest supports sealing the records, a substantially probability exists that the overriding interest if the records are not sealed, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. As such, sealing of the confidential materials is proper pursuant to Rule of Court 2.550. No opposition was filed. The prevailing party should prepare a proposed order that complies with Rule of Court 2.550(e) and includes a list of the specific documents that are permitted to be sealed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BRANDEN E. BICKEL VS. ORIENTAL WAREHOUSE OWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Granting Pltff Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 4, 2013, Line 25, PLAINTIFF BRANDEN BICKEL Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Granting Pltff Leave To File A First Amended Complaint Off calendar. Per the Re-Notice of Motion filed on August 22, 2013, the motion has been continued to September 18, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH L. SCHATZ VS. ELLIOT B. EVERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 4, 2013, Line 7: Defendant ELLIOT EVERS' Motion for Protective Order Continued to September 11, 2013 to give Mr. Evers the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Evers must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 4, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)
